title: Tridal: A multiplayer collaborative game
short description: Tridal is a multiplayer, cooperative game that advocates for responsible land use.
long description: Tridal is a multiplayer, cooperative environment that advocates responsible land use. The game is built with socket.io and vanilla javascript using the FAO's statistical database across categories like 'Forestry Production and Trade’, and ‘Temperature change on land’. Tridal reprioritises current resources– forests, farms, urban land, etc. – using mechanics that shift thinking paradigms. Every in-game action has multitudinous consequences, illustrating the scale of human damage. This project was supported by Rhizome Microgrants 2023.
role: Project management, prototyping user testing,  coding, fabrication, academic writing
image description: The game was formed from multiple stages of user testing, development and recalibration using low-fidelity, physical, grey box and high-fidelity prototypes.

image.text: Playing Tridal at Entropy: BFA/MFA Design & Technology Thesis Show.
Each in-game action has multiple repurcussions.
The land data was drawn from FAO's statistical database and players have to try and beat the rising sea levels

video description: This video is the detailed explanation of the game as presented at the Entropy symposium, moderated by Paolo Pedercini (Molleindustria).

title: Neo-TCH 1.3: Wearable Technology
short description: Neo-TCH 1.3 uses wearable tech to build a tactile, experimental, and performative installation piece.
long description: Neo-TCH 1.3 uses wearable tech to build a tactile, experimental, and performative installation piece. With soft sensors and found materials, we created a garment that was an intervention model in a speculative future. This piece challenges the importance of touch to the human experience and considers different ways of reimagining connection. Visit the website for an in-depth explanation and further documentation.
role: Project development, circuit testing & coding, website development
image description: The fabrication of the project involved lots of sewing, crocheting and stitching both conductive and not conductive materials to form the garment. We also need to do a lot of soldering, circuit testing and debugging to build the computational aspects.

image.text: Sensors built with conductive thread and foraged waste materials to encourage subjects to touch one another
Garments were built as an intervention model to improve non-verbal communication
The project was exhibited at enter here_ exhibition at the Pixelmouth gallery in NYC (2024)

title: Reservoirs
short description: Experiments in the poetics of display technology: Esoteric explorations of screens that enable unexpected and tactile encounters with rivers. Exploring embedded screens in objects, viewers can peer into a river within a vase to have an intimate encounter with the water.
long description: Experiments in the poetics of display technology: Esoteric explorations of screens that enable unexpected and tactile encounters with rivers. Exploring embedded screens in objects, viewers can peer into a river within a vase to have an intimate encounter with the water.

title: I'm not sure how to name home anymore
short description: 'I'm not sure how to name home anymore' is about how most of us live across many homes, tiny bits of our souls traced back to each place.
long description: While attending Worlds in Conversation, I was at a crossroads in my personal life. Within three weeks I had to pinpoint wether to move back to Bangalore or not. Suffice it to say, I was thinking about home a lot. Specifically, I was thinking about how most of us live across many homes, tiny bits of our souls traced back to each place. I wanted to converse about the many homes we've inhabited and the stories we carry. This game also, more crucially, reflected the wayfinding once you've outgrown a home. It embodied my decision-making and my steps to a new home.

image.text: Making something tactile was important since I was tired of pixel pushing and staring at a screen. Constructing this game was a labour of love.
This game is a worldbuilding game with a small ruleset. Players should feel free to make it their own.

title: Consilience: AI Driven Learning Tool
short description: A Question-first AI canvas tool that uses interconnected fields of study to enable exploring, falling into rabbit holes and associative learning.
long description: A Question-first AI canvas tool that uses interconnected fields of study to enable exploring, falling into rabbit holes and associative learning.

title: Forest Clearing
short description: A Forest Clearing is a collaborative multiplayer game where players grow and maintain their forest.
long description: A Forest Clearing is a collaborative multiplayer experience built using the p5 party library where players grow and maintain their forest. In this gamified view on deforestation, players are challenged by an increasing number of NPC lumberjacks that cut down the trees. The code is open-source and the game can be played in the browser.
role: Game development, front-end development, asset creation
image description: Some of the challenges of building this game involved making sure all the player were on the same page - this required multiple iterations of how we were conveying information and instruction. These are some of the final screens of the game.

image.alt: Plant more trees and grow the forest
image.text: Plant more trees and grow the forest

title: C S Soch: Visual Identity
short description: Visual assets for an online program for primary school children to develop computational thinking skills through coding games, stories and puzzles.
long description: CS Soch is an online program for primary school children to develop computational thinking skills through coding games, stories and puzzles. My brief was to create visual assets featuring quintessential Indian animals and landscapes familiar to young users. I worked with the company early on to build their visuals– game elements, character designs and the mascot. My illustrations informed the brand identity and company design system.
image description: These are some of the assets I worked on for games and puzzles.

title: Sheepish
short description: You are a border collie (woof) and you have only one job: herd all the sheep back into the pen! Call your other border collie friends to help you out and try to get all them sheeps fenced before the time runs out.
long description: You are a border collie (woof) and you have only one job: herd all the sheep back into the pen! Call your other border collie friends to help you out and try to get all them sheeps fenced before the time runs out.

title: Silver Lining
short description: In Silver Lining, players had to draw non-intersecting lines. It is an abstraction of simple paper games I played in school while pretending to take notes.

title: Rolling Stones
short description:
Prompt: Create a website that is a proverb. Project: 'A rolling stone gathers no moss'
The website is the rolling stone within this metaphor, when you stop rolling, aka scrolling, some moss appears on the screen and the content fades away.The website content chronicles the usage of the proverb in rock music.
long description:
Prompt: Create a website that is a proverb. Project: 'A rolling stone gathers no moss'
The website is the rolling stone within this metaphor, when you stop rolling, aka scrolling, some moss appears on the screen and the content fades away.The website content chronicles the usage of the proverb in rock music.

title: Q-sweeper
short description: Q-sweeper (quantum minesweeper) applies quantum concepts (noise and entanglement) and visualises data from the Bernstein-Vazirani algorithm to enhance the player's understanding of quantum mechanics.
long description: Q-sweeper (quantum minesweeper) applies quantum concepts (noise and entanglement) and visualises data from the Bernstein-Vazirani algorithm to enhance the player's understanding of quantum mechanics.

title: b[q]attleship
short description: B[q]attleship is a quantum version of the traditional board game battleship. It builds upon the ideas of Q-sweeper in a multiplayer context using the p5.party library to create a two-player game played across computers.
long description: B[q]attleship is a quantum version of the traditional board game battleship. It builds upon the ideas of Q-sweeper in a multiplayer context using the p5.party library to create a two-player game played across computers.

title: Animals
short description: These illustrations are inspired by imagery found in a story about Buddhist teachings, titled 'Roaring Bulls With No Fight'. Both illustrations highlight the unthinking consumption of one's surrounding. They chronicle living on a damaged land and being in a perpetuating, vicious cycle of harm.
long description: These illustrations are inspired by imagery found in a story about Buddhist teachings, titled 'Roaring Bulls With No Fight'. Both illustrations highlight the unthinking consumption of one's surrounding. They chronicle living on a damaged land and being in a perpetuating, vicious cycle of harm.

title: Tetris -2.0
short description: Tetris -2.0 is a typographic reimagining of the classic Tetris game, where letterforms serve as the falling blocks. The letters were constructed from a 9x9 matrix system, allowing for rotation and movement while stacking them.
long description: Tetris -2.0 is a typographic reimagining of the classic Tetris game, where letterforms serve as the falling blocks. The letters were constructed from a 9x9 matrix system, allowing for rotation and movement while stacking them.

title: Validata.js
short description: Validata.js is a data visualisation library that easily creates charts from CSV files. It is built as an add-on to p5.js to help new coders quickly make fun data-viz.
long description: Validata.js is a data visualisation library that easily creates charts from CSV files. It is built as an add-on to p5.js to help new coders quickly make fun data-viz.

title: Night Sky
short description: This was a personal project and an introduction to animation. I created this animation loop to celebrate a friend's birthday using after-effects for the first time.
long description: This was a personal project and an introduction to animation. I created this animation loop to celebrate a friend's birthday using after-effects for the first time.

long description: This is a collection of interactive words animated using p5.js. Interactions reflect the meaning of the specific word.

title: Touch me not
short description: The 'Touch me not' is something enticing that you want to touch, even though you probably should not. It emphasises how we can reach out to someone/thing, against their wishes, just because it elicits a response.
long description: The 'Touch me not' is something enticing that you want to touch, even though you probably should not. It emphasises how we can reach out to someone/thing, against their wishes, just because it elicits a response.

title: ID Card
short description: This project was my introduction to working with typography. The monogram manipulates the typeface Vortice to form my initials: TM. Using a light colour palette and sharp geometric lines, I made a student ID card that seamlessly incorporated the monogram.
long description: This project was my introduction to working with typography. The monogram manipulates the typeface Vortice to form my initials: TM. Using a light colour palette and sharp geometric lines, I made a student ID card that seamlessly incorporated the monogram.

title: Taskmaster Newsletter
short description: Taskmaster is a British panel show that has 5 comedians participate in a series of absurd tasks. The show's tone is nonsensical. For my newsletter, I wanted to pay homage to the show.
long description: Taskmaster is a British panel show that has 5 comedians participate in a series of absurd tasks. The show's tone is nonsensical. For my newsletter, I wanted to pay homage to the show.

title: Beta Game
short description: Beta is an autobiographical story based on a picturebook collection.
long description: Beta is an autobiographical story based on a picturebook collection.

title: Manifesto design
short description: For a class on typography, I had to select a manifesto and design an all typographical poster, that interprets the text and amplifies the text’s voice, tone, and word selection.
long description: For a class on typography, I had to select a manifesto and design an all typographical poster, that interprets the text and amplifies the text’s voice, tone, and word selection.

title: Central Park Paintings
short description: A series of watercolor paintings focussing on textures I saw at Central Park
long description: A series of watercolor paintings focussing on textures I saw at Central Park

title: Central Park Collage
short description: In New York, we are surrounded by buildings, pollution, people and constructions. Amongst this construction, finding a natural spot seems like a breath of fresh air. However, these spots, like parks and gardens, have also been constructed.' My project focussed on Central Park: a seemingly untainted natural space that's a product of intense construction and gentrification.
long description: In New York, we are surrounded by buildings, pollution, people and constructions. Amongst this construction, finding a natural spot seems like a breath of fresh air. However, these spots, like parks and gardens, have also been constructed.' My project focussed on Central Park: a seemingly untainted natural space that's a product of intense construction and gentrification.

title: Project Last Straw
short description: Project Last Straw is a zine that imagines the tenth avatar of Vishnu. In the zine, his last avatar is in the form of an AI virus using technological interfaces to control and communicate with us. Vishnu’s pessimism and resigned tone in the zine mirror a bleak world. Writing this zine was my reckoning with how pervasive and god-like technology is becoming.
long description: Project Last Straw is a zine that imagines the tenth avatar of Vishnu. In the zine, his last avatar is in the form of an AI virus using technological interfaces to control and communicate with us. Vishnu’s pessimism and resigned tone in the zine mirror a bleak world. Writing this zine was my reckoning with how pervasive and god-like technology is becoming.

title: Mapping Time
short description: The time I spend during the night seems elastic, stretching for hours before I go to sleep. I wanted to capture the stillness of the night as it defied all logical forms of time-keeping.
long description: The time I spend during the night seems elastic, stretching for hours before I go to sleep. I wanted to capture the stillness of the night as it defied all logical forms of time-keeping.
